data.title: Polygon Surges as Small Transactions Exceed 500,000 in November
data.description:
Polygon's small transactions see a 23% rise in November, hitting 500,000 as it becomes a crypto payment mainstay.
source: https://coincu.com/news/polygon-small-transactions-500000-november/

data.description:

data.title: H100 Appoints New Marketing and Communications Manager
data.description: According to Foresight News, Nordic Bitcoin treasury company H100 has announced the appointment of Elise Nolsen as its new Marketing and Communications Manager. Elise brings strategic marketing and sales experience from her previous role at Norwegian SaaS unicorn company Meltwater and has also worked with agencies in Norway.

data.title: Bank of Russia amends rules to allow crypto investments through mutual funds
data.description:
Russia’s central bank is lifting restrictions on crypto investments via mutual funds by amending regulations governing their activities.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bank-russia-amends-rules-crypto-mutual-funds/

data.title: Is the BTC downturn over? Signals still point to market weakness
data.description:
BTC recovered from $80,000 to $88,000, leading traders to believe the price bottom was reached.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/is-btc-downturn-over-signals-market-weakness/

data.title: Thailand imposes a 0% capital gains tax on Bitcoin traded through its national exchange.
data.description: The Bitcoin Historian posted on the X platform that Thailand currently imposes a 0% capital gains tax on Bitcoin traded through the national exchange.

data.title: Significant Transfer of TON Tokens to Elector Contract
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham reveals that at 18:22, a total of 2,001,158.284 TON tokens, valued at approximately $3,121,806.92, were transferred from an anonymous address beginning with Uf_rBQT to TON's Elector Contract.

data.title: Thailand Implements 0% Capital Gains Tax on Bitcoin Transactions
data.description: According to Odaily, The Bitcoin Historian shared on the X platform that Thailand currently imposes a 0% capital gains tax on Bitcoin traded through the national exchange.

data.title: The Bull And Bear Scenario For XRP That Could Play Out In November
data.description:
After a few months of ups and downs, the XRP price had fallen below $2 this month for the first time in seven months, breaking down toward its yearly support of $1. 79. While there has been some recovery recently, the momentum remains low, and the chances of a sustained recovery decline with each new dip. As the altcoin continues to struggle, a market analyst has outlined the two major directions that the price could go in, given the bull and bear scenarios
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/bull-anbear-scenario-for-xrp/

data.title: Bitplanet Increases Bitcoin Holdings with Recent Purchase
data.description: According to BlockBeats, South Korean publicly traded company Bitplanet (049470.KQ) has acquired an additional 28.5 bitcoins. This purchase brings the company's total bitcoin holdings to 228.5 BTC, as disclosed by BTCtreasuries.

data.title: Bitwise launches BWOW after Dogecoin fans demand an ETF
data.description:
Bitwise launches the BWOW Dogecoin ETF on NYSE Arca, giving investors regulated DOGE exposure as demand and competing ETF filings grow.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bitwise-launches-bwow-after-demand-of-etf/

data.title: BIS Appoints Tommaso Mancini-Griffoli to Lead Innovation Hub
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has announced the appointment of Tommaso Mancini-Griffoli as the head of the BIS Innovation Hub, effective March 2026. Mancini-Griffoli, a prominent economist in the field of digital money, will spearhead efforts to explore technological solutions within the central bank community, focusing on innovation. His role is expected to encompass ongoing work on central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), tokenized assets, and new forms of market infrastructure.

Mancini-Griffoli currently holds the position of assistant director in the International Monetary Fund’s Monetary and Capital Markets Department, where he leads initiatives related to payments and currencies. He is recognized as one of the IMF’s leading advocates for regulated and publicly backed digital money models and has previously highlighted the risks associated with unregulated stablecoins. The appointment comes as the BIS Innovation Hub intensifies its major projects, expanding its influence across global centers. The Hub has become a testing ground for blockchain-inspired settlement systems and digital currency prototypes, signaling a potential shift towards regulated tokenized money in the crypto space.

Mancini-Griffoli has been a key representative of the IMF at global policy forums on CBDCs and payments, frequently advocating for hybrid or public-backed arrangements as the most stable path forward, rather than fully private tokens. In 2020, he proposed that a synthetic private-public partnership CBDC could empower the private sector, including blockchain-backed stablecoins, to innovate. He has championed the concept of synthetic CBDCs, where private institutions issue digital money fully backed by central bank reserves, blending public-sector safety with private-sector innovation. Additionally, he supports tokenized financial instruments, provided they operate within a public-money architecture that ensures systemic stability and settlement finality.

In September, Mancini-Griffoli expressed concerns through an essay about the structural risks of stablecoins if not backed by safe assets and strong governance. He warned that poorly regulated issuers could expose users to runs, liquidity mismatches, and loss of value. The BIS Innovation Hub is currently engaged in several high-profile digital currency experiments, including the cross-border CBDC settlement network mBridge, the tokenized deposit infrastructure Agora, and real-time payments and interoperable CBDC rails known as Project Nexus. These initiatives reflect the BIS’s commitment to reimagining traditional finance with blockchain-inspired architecture. Under Mancini-Griffoli’s leadership, the innovation hub is set to accelerate high-impact initiatives, ranging from cross-border payment networks to tokenized deposits and interoperable CBDCs.

data.title: Digital Currency Fund to Debut on Hong Kong Stock Exchange in 2025
data.description: According to Foresight News, the Huaxia Hong Kong Dollar Digital Currency Fund, with the stock code 3471, is set to be listed and traded on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on November 28, 2025.

data.title: SoftBank has lost over $100 billion since it dumped Nvidia for OpenAI
data.description:
SoftBank has lost over $100 billion in market value since October after betting big on OpenAI and ditching Nvidia.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/softbank-has-lost-over-100-billion-to-openai/

data.title: Half of Polygon's payment transactions are small transfers between $10 and $100, with November's volume exceeding 500,000 transactions, a 23% increase month-over-month.
data.description: Leon Waidmann stated in an article on the X platform that half of all payment transactions on Polygon are small transfers worth between $10 and $100. According to data from major payment providers on Polygon (including Coinbase Commerce, Moonpay, Revolut, Rain, Transak, Paxos, NOWPayments, etc.), the number of small payment transactions alone exceeded 500,000 in November 2025, a 23% increase compared to October.
